meta.sprintTitle: Template Versioning v1 - Publish Snapshots, Rollback, and Historical Rendering Safety
meta.goal: Introduce immutable template layout versions and rollback controls so new storybooks can adopt improved layouts while existing books remain pinned to historically safe template snapshots.

basedOnPriorSprints: Sprint 50 - Admin Console v1 - Template Management
Sprint 7 - Layout to PDF Renderer v1
whyNow: Once admins can edit layouts visually, publishing those edits safely requires immutable version snapshots
Existing books should not silently change appearance when a template layout is improved
Rollback is needed to recover quickly from a bad layout publish without destructive data edits
productContext: Template versions are part of rendering safety, not just admin bookkeeping
Storybooks, previews, exports, and retries all need to know which template version they rely on
Versioning should make future per-page layout switching safer by preserving compatibility checks

nonGoals: Full Git-like diff UI with arbitrary merge workflows
Automatic migration of all existing storybooks to the newest template version
dependencies: Sprint 50 template management permissions and lifecycle
Sprint 52 builder draft and preview flows

operational: Each publish creates an immutable template version snapshot
Admin can roll back the active published version without destructive edits
Existing books continue to render against their pinned template version
product: Template iteration becomes safer and reversible
New books use the latest published version while historical books remain stable
Rollback can be performed in minutes during a template incident
quality: Preview, Studio, and export can resolve template versions deterministically
Version lineage and publish metadata are visible to admins
Retrying exports uses the correct template version policy
security: Only authorised roles can publish or roll back template versions
Historical versions are immutable once published
Rollback actions are fully audit logged

personas.name: Content Admin
personas.description: Publishes template changes and needs safe version history.

personas.name: Support Admin
personas.description: Needs visibility into which template version a project/export used.

inScope: Rollback action to activate a previous published version
Pinning storybooks/pages to template version provenance
Audit logging for publish and rollback
outOfScope: Automatic migration wizard for all existing books

rules: templates.publish is required to create a published snapshot
templates.rollback is required to reactivate an earlier version
Version history is read-only for templates.view roles
Published versions must be immutable after creation

suggestedEntities.name: template_versions
suggestedEntities.purpose: Immutable snapshots of template metadata and layout JSON at publish time

suggestedEntities.name: template_publish_pointer
suggestedEntities.purpose: Tracks which published version is currently active for new books

functionalRequirements.title: Immutable publish snapshot
requirements: Publishing a template creates an immutable snapshot containing metadata and layout JSON
Published snapshot receives a monotonically increasing version number
Snapshot cannot be edited in place after publication

functionalRequirements.title: Active publish pointer
requirements: System tracks the currently active published version for new storybooks
Changing the active pointer must not mutate historical snapshots
Only one active published version may exist per template at a time

functionalRequirements.title: Rollback
requirements: Authorised admins can reactivate a previous published version as the active version
Rollback should not delete newer versions
Rollback action must record who triggered it and why if a reason is supplied

functionalRequirements.title: Historical rendering safety
requirements: Storybooks created or populated against a version remain pinned to that version unless explicitly migrated
Exports and export retries can resolve the correct version for a project
Admin support views should surface template version provenance

functionalRequirements.title: Version history UI
requirements: Template detail should display version history with publish timestamps and active status
Admins should see which version is active and which versions are historical

recommendedEndpoints.purpose: List published template versions and active pointer
Create immutable published snapshot from current draft

technicalTasks.title: Define template version entities and contracts
technicalTasks.description: Create immutable snapshot model, active pointer logic, and DTOs.

technicalTasks.title: Implement publish snapshot flow
technicalTasks.description: Create server-side publish action that snapshots current draft and updates active pointer safely.

technicalTasks.title: Implement rollback flow
technicalTasks.description: Allow authorised admins to reactivate a previous published version.

technicalTasks.title: Version-aware storybook and export provenance
technicalTasks.description: Persist and surface template version references for Studio pages and exports.

technicalTasks.title: Build version history UI
technicalTasks.description: Show version history and active state inside template admin detail.

technicalTasks.title: Testing
technicalTasks.description: Add coverage for immutable versioning, rollback, and historical rendering safety.

acceptanceCriteria.given: a draft template ready to publish
acceptanceCriteria.then: an immutable template version snapshot is created and becomes the active published version

acceptanceCriteria.given: a storybook created on an older template version
acceptanceCriteria.when: a newer template version is published
acceptanceCriteria.then: the existing storybook remains pinned to its original version unless explicitly migrated

acceptanceCriteria.when: a super_admin rolls back to a prior version
acceptanceCriteria.then: the prior version becomes active for new books without deleting the newer snapshot

acceptanceCriteria.given: a support/admin operator viewing a project or export
acceptanceCriteria.then: the template id and version used for rendering are visible

risks.risk: Version resolution differs across Studio, preview, and export
risks.mitigation: Use one version resolution contract shared across all render paths
Keep published snapshots immutable and change only the active pointer

risks.risk: Projects created before versioning lack provenance
risks.mitigation: Provide sensible fallback provenance rules and migration notes for legacy records

futureSprintHooks: Future: visual diff for template versions
Future: project migration between template versions
Future: per-page layout switching compatibility checks

definitionOfDone: System tracks one active published version per template
Rollback flow exists with permissions and audit logging
Storybooks and exports can resolve template provenance
Version history is visible in admin UI
Automated tests cover publish, rollback, and historical rendering safety
